steps.title: Use the purpose note
steps.content: The student wants to specify the reason the Mayflower Compact was created. The notes give that reason directly: it was created in 1620 to establish a common government. The sentence should focus on that purpose, not on Samuel Carver, the number of signatories, or the quoted goal of planting a colony.

steps.title: Remove irrelevant details
steps.content: Choice A describes the Compact as a legal agreement and quotes what the signatories wanted, but it does not state why the agreement was created. Choice B gives the date and the same colony quotation, again without explaining the purpose. Choice D identifies the 41 signatories and Samuel Carver's later role, which are background facts rather than the requested reason.

steps.title: Choose the purpose sentence
steps.content: Choice C states that the Mayflower Compact was created to establish a common government among the pilgrims who immigrated to Plymouth Colony. That sentence directly uses the relevant note and accomplishes the student's goal without adding unrelated background. The answer is C.
